content:
# Joshua Chapter 18 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 241-242 | **Source Image:** `../../source-texts/hebrew-OT/JOS_018_page_001.jpg`

1. And the whole congregation of the Sons of Israel assembled at Shiloh, and they set up the Tent of Meeting there. And the land was subdued before them.

2. But there remained among the Sons of Israel seven tribes who had not yet divided their inheritance.

3. And Joshua said to the Sons of Israel: "How long will you be slack in going to possess the land that YHWH, the God of your fathers, has given you?

4. Appoint for yourselves three men from each tribe, and I will send them, and they shall arise and walk through the land and write a description of it according to their inheritance, and they shall come to me.

5. And they shall divide it into seven portions. Judah shall stay in his territory on the south, and the house of Joseph shall stay in their territory on the north.

6. And you shall write a description of the land in seven portions and bring it here to me. And I will cast lots for you here before YHWH our God.

7. For the Levites have no portion among you, for the priesthood of YHWH is their inheritance. And Gad and Reuben and the half-tribe of Manasseh have received their inheritance beyond the Jordan to the east, which Moses the servant of YHWH gave to them."

8. And the men arose and went. And Joshua commanded those who went to write a description of the land, saying: "Go and walk through the land and write a description of it and return to me, and I will cast lots for you here before YHWH in Shiloh."

9. And the men went and passed through the land and wrote a description of it by cities in seven portions in a scroll. And they came to Joshua at the camp in Shiloh.

10. And Joshua cast lots for them in Shiloh before YHWH, and there Joshua divided the land to the Sons of Israel according to their divisions.

11. And the lot of the tribe of the sons of Benjamin came up according to their clans. And the territory of their lot fell between the sons of Judah and the sons of Joseph.

12. And their border on the north side was from the Jordan, and the border went up to the side of Jericho on the north, and went up through the hill country westward, and its end was at the wilderness of Beth-aven.

13. And the border passed from there to Luz, to the side of Luz — that is, Bethel — southward. And the border went down to Ataroth-addar, upon the mountain that is south of Lower Beth-horon.

14. And the border was drawn and turned on the west side southward, from the mountain that is before Beth-horon southward, and its end was at Kiriath-baal — that is, Kiriath-jearim — a city of the sons of Judah. This was the west side.

15. And the south side was from the edge of Kiriath-jearim, and the border went out westward, and it went out to the spring of the waters of Nephtoah.

16. And the border went down to the edge of the mountain that is before the Valley of Ben-hinnom, which is in the Valley of Rephaim northward. And it went down to the Valley of Hinnom, to the side of the Jebusite southward, and went down to En-rogel.

17. And it was drawn from the north and went out to En-shemesh, and went out to Geliloth, which is opposite the Ascent of Adummim. And it went down to the Stone of Bohan the son of Reuben.

18. And it passed to the side opposite the Arabah northward, and went down to the Arabah.

19. And the border passed to the side of Beth-hoglah northward, and the end of the border was at the northern bay of the Salt Sea, at the southern end of the Jordan. This was the south border.

20. And the Jordan was its border on the east side. This was the inheritance of the sons of Benjamin, by its borders all around, according to their clans.

21. And the cities of the tribe of the sons of Benjamin according to their clans were: Jericho, and Beth-hoglah, and Emek-keziz,

22. and Beth-arabah, and Zemaraim, and Bethel,

23. and Avvim, and Parah, and Ophrah,

24. and Chephar-ammoni, and Ophni, and Geba — twelve cities and their villages.

25. Gibeon, and Ramah, and Beeroth,

26. and Mizpeh, and Chephirah, and Mozah,

27. and Rekem, and Irpeel, and Taralah,

28. and Zela, Eleph, and the Jebusite — that is, Jerusalem — Gibeah, and Kiriath — fourteen cities and their villages. This was the inheritance of the sons of Benjamin according to their clans.

---
## LOGOS Translation Notes

1. **YHWH (יהוה):** Rendered consistently as "YHWH" throughout, preserving the Tetragrammaton as written in the Leningrad Codex rather than substituting "the LORD." This is a core LOGOS Bible convention.

2. **"Sons of Israel" (בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל):** Rendered literally per LOGOS convention (vv. 1, 2, 3, 10) rather than the interpretive "Israelites" or "children of Israel."

3. **"sons of Benjamin" (בְּנֵי בִנְיָמִן):** Similarly rendered literally throughout (vv. 11, 20, 21, 28) to maintain consistency with the "sons of" convention for tribal designations.

4. **"Tent of Meeting" (אֹהֶל מוֹעֵד):** The standard formal rendering for the portable sanctuary (v. 1), preserving the Hebrew sense of the appointed place of divine encounter.

5. **Shiloh (שִׁלֹה):** The assembly site where Israel gathered and the tabernacle was established (vv. 1, 8, 9, 10). This marks a pivotal transition from Gilgal as Israel's base of operations to Shiloh, which will serve as the central sanctuary until the time of Samuel.

6. **"slack" (מִתְרַפִּים, mithrappim):** From the root רפה (raphah), meaning "to be slack, to be lazy." Joshua's pointed question in v. 3 rebukes the remaining seven tribes for their delay. "Slack" preserves the force of the Hebrew hitpael participle.

7. **"scroll" (סֵפֶר, sepher):** In v. 9, the men wrote the land description "in a scroll" (עַל־סֵפֶר). While *sepher* can mean "book, document, scroll," the formal rendering "scroll" reflects the physical writing medium of the period.

8. **"lots" (גוֹרָל, goral):** The casting of lots (vv. 6, 8, 10) was understood as a divinely guided method of allocation, performed "before YHWH" — indicating that the distribution was not arbitrary but theocratic.

9. **Valley of Ben-hinnom (גֵּי בֶן־הִנֹּם):** Rendered literally in v. 16 as "Valley of Ben-hinnom" (= "Valley of the Son of Hinnom"), preserving the personal name. This is the valley that later became associated with idolatrous practices and the concept of *Gehenna*.

10. **City list variations:** The Hebrew in vv. 21-28 lists Benjamin's cities in two groups: twelve cities (v. 24) and fourteen cities (v. 28), totaling twenty-six. Some place names are difficult to vocalize with certainty from the consonantal text; renderings follow the Masoretic vocalization of the Leningrad Codex.

11. **"the Jebusite" (הַיְבוּסִי):** In v. 28, "the Jebusite — that is, Jerusalem" identifies the pre-Israelite name for the city. The singular collective "the Jebusite" is retained rather than "the Jebusites" to reflect the Hebrew gentilic form used here as a place reference.

12. **"the house of Joseph" (בֵּית יוֹסֵף):** In v. 5, this phrase encompasses the combined territory of Ephraim and Manasseh (west). Rendered literally rather than expanded to "the tribes of Ephraim and Manasseh."
